title: basenameOf returns original string on degenerate path input (/ or empty)
description:
PR-review third-pass (silent-failure-hunter, post-commit 1256605): basenameOf in BrowseTab.svelte applyDrawerDiff (helper function) splits on path separators and returns segments[segments.length - 1] if non-empty, otherwise the original path string. On degenerate inputs ('/' or ''), this returns the original string and surfaces it in the user-facing success banner as e.g. '0 steering ( )' or '0 steering (/)'.

Not security-sensitive — the steering install layer validates paths upstream, so this shape is structurally near-impossible to reach today. UX nit only; flagging because the same defensive pattern is already established in format.ts.

File: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/components/BrowseTab.svelte (basenameOf helper inside applyDrawerDiff).
design:
Mirror the '<unnamed>' sentinel precedent from formatSkippedSkill in crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/format.ts:70. When segments.length === 0 (or all segments are empty after split-and-filter), return '<unknown>' instead of the raw path. Optional debug! / console.debug log at the call site so a real reproducer would leave a breadcrumb.

Trivial change (~2 lines). Could be bundled with kiro-k5hj's BrowseTab batch-helper extraction if that lands first.
acceptance_criteria: basenameOf('/') and basenameOf('') return '<unknown>'. Existing happy-path inputs unchanged.

title: Test gaps: DuplicateNameSpaceExhausted, save from_name validation, wrapper happy paths, concurrent stress
description: Surfaced by PR #120 pr-test-analyzer + marketplace-security-reviewer (I16). Coverage gaps remaining after slice 1: (1) DuplicateNameSpaceExhausted exhaustion path is not exercised — a bug that off-by-ones the loop bound or swaps the variant's source_name/cap fields would not be caught (project.rs:1530 SANITY_CAP=10000). (2) save_user_agent's from_name validation is untested — only create_user_agent's name validation is covered; a refactor that drops from_name validation and accepts a traversal-shaped from_name would not be caught. (3) Three of five Tauri wrappers (create_user_agent, save_user_agent, duplicate_user_agent) have no happy-path tests — only list_user_agents and delete_user_agent are covered. Arg-order regressions or body-bytes corruption would not be caught at the wrapper level. (4) Concurrent-stress tests are entirely absent: no spawn-two-threads test for create/create, create/install, delete/install on the same agent name; the new with_file_lock work in C3/C4/I6 would not catch a regression that bypasses the lock.
design: Per gap. (1) Parameterize SANITY_CAP behind a private const that can be lowered under cfg(test), or introduce duplicate_user_agent_inner(svc, cap) — test seeds k=1 and k=2, calls with cap=2, asserts DuplicateNameSpaceExhausted with the right source_name and cap fields. (2) Three lines mirroring create_user_agent_rejects_path_separator — call save_user_agent with from_name='../escape', assert InvalidName. (3) Three #[tokio::test] cases — one per uncovered wrapper, happy-path only, asserting the file lands on disk OR the returned String matches expected. (4) Add a #[cfg(unix)] concurrent_stress module that uses std::thread::spawn + Arc<Barrier> to drive two threads through create_user_agent('foo', ...) on the same project and asserts exactly one succeeds with deterministic content. Cross-platform alternative: use rayon::join. Coordinate with kiro-fd40 / kiro-sukr — once the wire types tighten, some of these tests get a stronger assertion target.

title: tauri.conf.json csp is null — set restrictive CSP
description: Surfaced by PR #120 tauri-ipc-auditor review (I10). crates/kiro-control-center/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json sets 'csp': null, leaving the renderer with no script-source restrictions. Pre-existing config — not introduced by PR #120 — but PR #120 widened the blast radius by adding 5 new write/delete primitives (the agents-authoring commands) callable from any script context the renderer executes. An XSS via a Markdown-rendered description, an SVG-icon plugin, or a compromised npm transitive could now call create_user_agent / save_user_agent / delete_user_agent / duplicate_user_agent in addition to the previously-existing install/remove surface.
design: Set a restrictive CSP like 'default-src self; script-src self' and tighten as the frontend bundle is audited. Test with cargo tauri dev that no inline-script / inline-style violations fire on the legitimate UI flows. If Vite emits inline scripts during dev that fail the CSP, scope the strict CSP to release config only via dev/prod conditional. Document the policy in a comment near the csp field. Affects all commands, not just agents-view.

title: agents-view slice 2: Tools section UI + tools-catalog.ts port
description:
Slice 2 of the agents-view feature. Builds on slice 1 (skeleton: list + editor shell + Identity + System Prompt). Adds the Tools section per design § 5 of Kiro Control Center Design System/design_handoff_agents/README.md (screenshots 04-tools.png, 04-tools-allowed-picker.png).

Scope:
- Port agents-data.js AGENT_TOOLS table to crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/agents/tools-catalog.ts as a static TS module (spec decision #5: static catalog from the design bundle, not a runtime query into kiro-cli).
- Add editor/ToolsPanel.svelte + editor/AllowedToolsList.svelte components per the Svelte component map in design_handoff_agents/README.md.
- Auto-allowed tools panel (top), Available tools grid grouped by category, External MCP tools group (bottom).
- Cross-section state cleanup: toggling a tool off MUST also remove it from allowedTools and toolAliases (spec 'Things to watch out for' item 8).
- Rail count badge: Tools N updates live from draft state.

No new Tauri commands. Reads/writes flow through slice 1's create_user_agent / save_user_agent (which take serde_json::Value, so tool fields round-trip without backend changes).
design:
Source-of-truth for the tool catalog is the static TS module. Categories (Filesystem, Code, Shell, Cloud, etc.) come from the design bundle's window.AGENT_TOOLS verbatim. The 'NOT VISIBLE' yellow chip in the auto-allowed picker is for tools not in the agent's tools array (i.e., not yet enabled). The '+ Add custom' free-text affordance is how @server/tool MCP entries land in the allow list (the slice 3 MCP UI does NOT subsume this — the allow-list is per-tool-name, MCP-added tools share the keyspace).

Falsifier idea for the catalog port: render every category in a unit test (vitest, since this is pure data), assert the row count matches the design bundle's AGENT_TOOLS Object.keys count. A future regression where a category is dropped during port would fail this.

title: Drawer post-apply refresh failure overwrites the success/failure summary
description: Surfaced in PR #N (post-list_plugin_catalog branch review 2026-05-15) by silent-failure-hunter. applyDrawerDiff (BrowseTab.svelte:945-951) wraps fetchCatalogFor() in try/catch and on failure sets installError = 'Post-apply refresh failed: ${reason}'. This wipes out the summary banner built between lines 790-800 — which may have legitimately said 'Installed: 3 skills, 2 agents · Warnings: ...'. The install itself succeeded; the user only sees the refresh failure with no record of what landed.
design: Either (a) append the refresh failure to the existing installMessage rather than overwriting (installError = installError ? `${installError} | Post-apply refresh failed: ${reason}` : ...), or (b) use a separate installWarning slot for the refresh failure so the success summary remains visible. (b) is cleaner: the install succeeded, the refresh is a separate operation, the warning slot reflects the actual semantic. Vitest if composition is extracted.

title: hook-post-edit: widen clippy scope to --workspace (currently --package <derived>)
description:
Surfaced during PR #120 (agents-view-slice-1). The post-edit hook at xtask/src/main.rs::run_clippy invokes:

    cargo clippy --package <derived> --no-deps --message-format=short -- -D warnings

This catches lints in the same package as the edited file but misses two classes of failure that CI's workspace-scope clippy DOES catch:

1. Cross-package lints that only fire when the whole workspace is compiled together (rare in practice, but possible — e.g., conflicting trait impls visible only at workspace-graph resolution).
2. Lints in OTHER packages that consume the edited package's public API. If editing a public type in kiro-market-core introduces a clippy violation at the kiro-control-center call site, the per-package hook on kiro-market-core misses it; the workspace-scope CI flags it.

Symptom on PR #120: clippy::map_unwrap_or and clippy::items_after_statements landed in slice S3's commit (9034a13) and slice S7's commit (70b3a09) respectively. Both edits ran through hook-post-edit's --package kiro-market-core clippy, both lints were package-local, BOTH should have been caught locally. They weren't surfaced visibly — see notes in the design about hook-output co-mingling — but the wider hypothesis is 'the hook isn't catching things CI catches.' Widening to --workspace eliminates the scope-difference uncertainty entirely.

Cost: clippy --workspace on a cold cache adds ~3× the wall-clock vs --package. On a warm cache (the common case for incremental edits) the delta shrinks to <2× because incremental clippy reuses analysis across the dependency graph. For a 60-second hook timeout the margin is comfortable; the existing PR #120 work hit clippy times of 2m43s for full workspace, well over the timeout.

Files: xtask/src/main.rs::run_clippy (line 425+), the args list at line 428-437. Plus the call site at xtask/src/main.rs::hook_post_edit (line 58+) — derive_package becomes unnecessary if the hook always invokes workspace clippy.
design:
Replace the run_clippy(pkg, workspace_dir) signature with run_clippy(workspace_dir) — drop the pkg parameter. Change the args from ['clippy', '--package', pkg, '--no-deps', '--message-format=short', '--', '-D', 'warnings'] to ['clippy', '--workspace', '--no-deps', '--message-format=short', '--', '-D', 'warnings'].

Bump the PostToolUse timeout in .claude/settings.json from 60 to 180 (workspace clippy on cold cache can run 90s+ on this codebase; the existing 60s would silently truncate).

derive_package logic stays in xtask (used by tests and as a sanity gate — if the file isn't in any workspace package, skip clippy entirely), but its result is no longer threaded into the clippy invocation.

Falsifier: edit a file in kiro-market-core that introduces a clippy::map_unwrap_or, confirm the hook surfaces the warning. Revert. Edit a file in kiro-control-center src-tauri that introduces a clippy violation in a different package via API change, confirm the hook still catches it (the case the current --package scope misses).

title: Concurrent refreshProjects() race — add scanGeneration counter
description:
refreshProjects() in crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/stores/project.svelte.ts sets store.scanning = true at entry and false in finally. Two concurrent calls (e.g. rapid scan-root add/remove) share the single boolean — the first's finally fires while the second is still mid-scan, prematurely flipping scanning back to false.

Failure mode: cosmetic only. Spinner disappears briefly while a scan is still in flight. No data corruption (the second call's result.discoveredProjects write happens last regardless of which scan finished first, which IS a separate issue but small).

Fix sketch: introduce a scanGeneration: number on store, increment at refreshProjects entry, snapshot the value, only flip scanning=false in finally if gen still matches snapshot. Also only write discoveredProjects on the most-recent generation's result.

Detected by: PR #115 review (silent-failure-hunter I1, code-reviewer concern). Rated cosmetic.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/stores/project.svelte.ts:85-110 (post-PR-114 catch block)

title: Promote InstallFilter::Names(&[String]) to Names(&NonEmptyNames) newtype in core
description:
PR-review third-pass (type-design-analyzer, post-commit 1256605): InstallFilter::Names(&'a [String]) currently accepts an empty slice, which is structurally ambiguous with InstallFilter::All at the core layer (filter_matches returns false for every item, surface_unmatched_names sees no misses → silent Ok). The Tauri layer guards against this via reject_empty_names in crates/kiro-control-center/src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs (added in commit 0e38e9f, prefix-pinned in 1256605), but the CLI consumers in crates/kiro-market/ have no equivalent guard.

Files:
- crates/kiro-market-core/src/service/mod.rs (InstallFilter enum, lines 103-121 post-1256605)
- crates/kiro-market-core/src/service/mod.rs (all install_* call sites consuming InstallFilter::Names)
- crates/kiro-control-center/src-tauri/src/commands/mod.rs (reject_empty_names — becomes TryFrom impl)
- crates/kiro-market/src/commands/install.rs (CLI install paths — gain the same guarantee)
design:
Introduce a slice newtype NonEmptyNames<'a>(&'a [String]) with a fallible TryFrom<&'a [String]>. Change InstallFilter::Names(&'a [String]) → Names(NonEmptyNames<'a>). Every existing call site that constructs Names must now go through TryFrom; the conversion failure is the same 'names list must not be empty' error reject_empty_names raises today.

reject_empty_names becomes a thin wrapper that calls TryFrom and maps the error to CommandError::Validation with the {command}: prefix the test in commands/mod.rs pins.

Specta exposure: NonEmptyNames lives on the install function signatures, not on any #[tauri::command] wire-format struct, so no specta::Type derivation needed. If a future command surfaces Names in a wire-format request type, derive specta::Type at that point.

Out of scope: making InstallFilter itself #[non_exhaustive] (separate concern, not blocked by this).
acceptance_criteria: Every call site that builds InstallFilter::Names goes through NonEmptyNames::try_from. reject_empty_names is a delegating wrapper. CLI install paths gain the same empty-names rejection the Tauri commands have today. Test coverage: a new core-level test pinning that NonEmptyNames::try_from(&[]) errors, plus the existing reject_empty_names_includes_command_prefix test continues to pass through the new delegation.

title: Per-item steering/agent install/remove Tauri commands for Customize drawer
description:
The BrowseTab redesign's Customize drawer (slice 4, shipped 2026-05-14) renders steering and agents sections as read-only because today the bindings only support whole-plugin install (installPlugin), whole-plugin steering install (installPluginSteering), per-skill install (installSkills), and per-skill remove (removeSkill). NO per-file steering install/remove and NO per-agent install/remove exist.

User-visible symptom (the trigger to prioritize this): a plugin that ships only steering and/or agents (no skills) opens in the drawer with every checkbox disabled. Slice 4 added an explanatory banner pointing users at the whole-plugin Install button as the workaround, but Customize on a steering-only plugin remains a dead-end UX-wise.

Required Tauri commands (signatures TBD during their own design pass):
- install_steering_files(marketplace, plugin, names: Vec<String>, force, project_path) -> InstallSteeringResult
- remove_steering_file(plugin, name, project_path) -> Result<(), CommandError>
- install_agent(marketplace, plugin, name, force, project_path) -> InstallAgentResult
- remove_agent(plugin, name, project_path) -> Result<(), CommandError>

Each requires a corresponding kiro-market-core service method, validated newtypes for fields crossing FFI, and the typical _impl(svc, ...) testable shape per CLAUDE.md.

Implementation shape mirrors slice 1 (which shipped list_plugin_catalog_for_marketplace via three new service methods + one Tauri command + a binding-shape fence). Estimated ~1 day of focused work for the four commands; the drawer wiring changes are an additional ~half-day to widen the diff shape and apply handler.

Once landed, the drawer's Steering and Agents sections become interactive (parallel to today's Skills section), the banner from slice 4 is removed, and skills-less plugins regain a meaningful Customize/Manage drawer.

Status: unblocked since slice 1 shipped (4601457 → 010c489 commit chain on main); the gating prereq is gone. Ready to start.

title: agents-view: surface JSON-name vs filename drift as UI warning
description:
Deferral from PR #120's Finding B response. The PR adopts 'filename stem as canonical identity' for the Agents list row, fixing the CRUD breakage that JSON-name-first surfaced. As a side effect, pre-existing drift (a file at .kiro/agents/foo.json containing {"name": "bar"}) becomes invisible to the list-page display — the row shows 'foo', not 'bar'.

Drift sources after PR #120 lands:
- Manual file edits outside Control Center (user opens foo.json in  and changes the JSON name field).
- Future kiro-cli versions that have a different filename-name policy.

NOT a drift source: the Control Center editor (slice S13 onward) — save_user_agent rejects mismatch via Finding B's option-2 enforcement.

Symptom when drift exists pre-PR-#120: row displays the JSON name, CRUD operations on that name fail mysteriously (delete is silent no-op, duplicate errors with SourceNotFound, save creates a NEW file alongside the original).

Symptom after PR #120: row displays the filename stem, CRUD works correctly, but the user's manual edit to the JSON name field is silently overridden visually.

The right UX is somewhere between 'always show JSON name (current pre-PR-120)' and 'silently hide drift (PR-120 fix)'. Likely shape: row.name = filename stem (canonical), PLUS a new wire-format field row.json_name_override: Option<String> populated when the JSON name field differs from filename stem. Frontend renders a small warning badge next to drifted rows.

Defer until: a real user hits the surprise. Until then, the silent-hide behavior is acceptable because the population of drifted files in any project is near-zero (no editor introduces them; only manual edits do).
design:
Two-part change:

Backend (kiro-market-core):
- Add  to UserAgentRow. Populated by list_user_agents iff the JSON file's name field is Some AND differs from the filename stem. None otherwise.
- Update the projection in try_project_user_agent_row to compute and populate the field.

Frontend (kiro-control-center):
- In AgentsTab.svelte row rendering: when row.json_name_override.is_some(), render a small warning badge (yellow, 'drifted name' or similar) with tooltip explaining the divergence.
- The badge's tooltip surfaces both names so the user can decide: rename file, edit JSON, or leave as-is.

Wire format: additive (new optional field on UserAgentRow). Specta will pick it up via bindings.ts regen. Existing serialized rows continue to deserialize cleanly with the field absent (serde default Option = None).

Falsifier: pre-write  with , call list_user_agents, assert returned row has name='foo' AND json_name_override=Some('bar'). Variant: same fixture without drift (name=stem) returns json_name_override=None. The new field's documentation explicitly references this issue's number so future-readers can navigate back to the rationale.

title: F1: Engine-level multi-conflict collection in classify_companion_collision
description:
Today classify_companion_collision (crates/kiro-market-core/src/project.rs:3106) returns the first conflict and bails. The wire-format field FailedAgent::CompanionBundle.conflicts is shape-compatible with multi-conflict (Vec<PathBuf>) but in practice ships length-0 or length-1 only.

Implementation: walk every rel_path before returning, accumulate every PathOwnedByOtherPlugin / OrphanFileAtDestination collision into a Vec, then return the accumulated set as a single AgentError variant or as a new collected-conflicts variant. companion_conflicts_from_error in service/mod.rs:739 already handles the projection; the engine change is independent.

Forward-compat: no wire-format migration required — the Vec already exists.

Why deferred from PR #113: independent engine work; the wire-format change shipped without it because the FE consumes conflicts as opaque list (length-1 today, length-N tomorrow).

See: docs/plans/2026-05-09-failed-agent-discriminator-design.md F1, crates/kiro-market-core/src/project.rs:3106-3171

title: hook-post-edit: replace single-file rustfmt with workspace-wide cargo fmt --all
description:
Surfaced during PR #120 (agents-view-slice-1). The post-edit hook at xtask/src/main.rs::run_rustfmt invokes:

    rustfmt <single-file-path>

This catches formatter drift on the file the agent just touched, but misses two cases that CI's workspace-wide 'cargo fmt --all --check' catches:

1. Pre-existing fmt drift on files no agent has touched in the current session. PR #120 hit this on commands/kiro_settings.rs and commands/steering.rs — neither file was modified by the agents-view work, both had drift accumulated on main, both failed CI Format. The hook never had a chance to reformat them because the trigger is per-edit.
2. Edits whose formatting depends on rustfmt config that's only honored under cargo's project-aware invocation (edition, custom rustfmt.toml settings, etc.). Bare 'rustfmt <file>' may not pick up all the same project context cargo fmt does. In practice the gap is usually small but real for projects with custom .rustfmt.toml.

Fix: replace the per-file rustfmt call with a workspace-wide 'cargo fmt --all'. This costs more wall-clock (workspace fmt on this repo is ~1-2s vs <100ms single-file) but is still negligible inside a 60s timeout, AND closes the drift-on-untouched-files gap.

Files: xtask/src/main.rs::run_rustfmt (line 399+), the Command::new('rustfmt') invocation at line 404.
design:
Replace the rustfmt single-file invocation with:

    Command::new("cargo")
        .current_dir(workspace_dir)
        .args(["fmt", "--all"])
        .status()

(or .output() if we want to surface stderr on failure). Drop the &Path argument since cargo fmt walks the workspace.

The single-file path is still useful for one thing: surfacing 'this exact file's syntax broke rustfmt' diagnostics. Cargo fmt --all on a syntax-broken file silently skips it. Compromise: run cargo fmt --all first, then run single-file rustfmt for the explicit purpose of surfacing per-file failures (the diagnostic shape that already exists at line 411-419). The double-invocation is cheap because cargo fmt --all already normalized the file's whitespace, so the single-file rustfmt is a no-op unless the file has a syntax error that cargo fmt skipped.

Falsifier: introduce trailing whitespace into a file the agent is NOT currently editing (e.g., echo ' ' >> crates/kiro-market/src/main.rs), then edit a different file. The hook should reformat both files — current behavior only fixes the edited one.

Cross-references the workspace-scope clippy widening tracked at kiro-w9z4. Both share the same 'per-file hook scope is too narrow' shape; if both are taken together, the hook becomes the workspace-wide gate it was conceptually meant to be.

title: PR #115 review polish backlog
description:
Nine 'nice to have' items surfaced during the multi-agent review of PR #115 (test/failed-agent-coverage-gaps). None is blocking; pick up opportunistically. All rated Minor/Suggestion by the reviewers.

These split into three rough categories:

1. UI race / display polish (FE):
   - Concurrent refreshProjects() race (scanGeneration counter)
   - ProjectPicker stale-data masking during new scan
   - ScanRootsPanel z-index conflict with global toast

2. Review-toolkit prompt / steering hygiene:
   - code-reviewer.md inconsistent mode-marker phrasings
   - code-reviewer.md cross-prompt references without file paths
   - plugin-actions.ts:138 InstalledTab cite without line number
   - DRY: hoist Specialist Output discipline block to steering doc
   - 'What changed from v1' sections cleanup (post-v2 settling)

3. Type-design polish:
   - FailedAgent variant naming asymmetry (Agent / UnparseableAgent / CompanionBundle reads as kinds-of-agent, not kinds-of-failure)

See: PR #115 multi-agent review aggregated report (referenced in /pr-review-toolkit:review-pr output from 2026-05-10)

title: Make print_agent_failure testable: refactor to accept a writer, then add per-arm tests
description: Decision-log N12 (PR #N second-pass review 2026-05-15) called for adding print_install_agents_* tests asserting each FailedAgent arm renders properly, defending the post-7dce903 fix that replaced {:?} (Debug) with explicit Display rendering. Implementation reveals print_agent_failure (install.rs:390-449) writes directly to eprintln! and crates/kiro-market has NO existing test module. Adding tests requires either (a) refactoring print_agent_failure to take &mut impl Write so tests pass a Vec<u8>, or (b) using a test harness that captures stderr. Both are structural changes that exceed the drift-cleanup commit's scope. The vitest analog (format.test.ts:377) already covers the UI rendering of FailedAgent variants; a CLI regression to Debug stays caught by the integration tests + human eyes but not by a focused unit test.
design: Refactor print_agent_failure (and the surrounding print_install_outcome / print_steering_outcome family) to take &mut dyn std::io::Write. The default callsite passes std::io::stderr().lock(); tests pass Vec<u8> and assert the rendered bytes. Mirror crates/kiro-market-core's existing test pattern of capturing structured output. Add tests asserting each FailedAgent arm renders a string that (a) contains the agent name OR source-path OR plugin name (whichever applies), (b) does NOT contain {kind: characters (would indicate Debug formatting regression), (c) for the non_exhaustive fallback arm, asserts the generic 'unrendered variant' message.

title: Add #[non_exhaustive] to PluginCatalogEntry / SteeringItemInfo / AgentItemInfo
description: PR-review S6 (type-design): the three new catalog wire types in crates/kiro-market-core/src/service/browse.rs have all-pub fields with no #[non_exhaustive] attribute. Adding a field is a breaking change for any workspace-internal caller using struct-literal construction (e.g., test fixtures in prove_it_list_plugin_catalog.rs). The companion type SkippedItem in the same file DOES carry #[non_exhaustive] — inconsistency is the smell. MarketplaceAddOptions (mod.rs:154-156) is the precedent for additive future-proofing.\n\nLow priority: SkillInfo (pre-existing) also lacks the attribute, so the inconsistency long-precedes this PR. The fix is small but the urgency is too.
design: Add #[non_exhaustive] to the three structs. Workspace-internal callers using struct-literal construction need '..' added (the prove_it_* tests are the only places this matters). Output types — external API isn't pattern-matching these for exhaustiveness, so the only cost is the call-site '..'.

title: AgentEditor path chip shows future path during rename, not on-disk path
description: Origin: PR #121 Gemini Code Assist inline review at AgentEditor.svelte:533 (timestamp 2026-05-23T02:42:07Z). The path chip at crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/components/AgentEditor.svelte:510-512 reads '.kiro/agents/{draftName}.json' where draftName is the live Identity-panel input. During a rename, draftName !== originalName — the chip shows the FUTURE path before the user clicks Save, implying the file has already moved. The on-disk file is still at '.kiro/agents/{originalName}.json' until performSaveEdit completes. Slight UX confusion (user wonders 'wait, did Save already fire?') and a subtle support-trap when the user looks at the chip path on disk and finds nothing there.
design: Two viable shapes: (a) show '{originalName}.json' until save commits, updating only after onSaved fires; (b) when draftName !== originalName, render a two-state indicator like '{originalName}.json -> {draftName}.json (pending rename)'. (b) is more informative — teaches the user what Save will do — and parallels the 'Rename to' verb the post-save toast uses. (a) is simpler but loses the new-name signal entirely. Recommend (b). Trigger condition: !isNew && originalName !== '' && draftName !== originalName. To make this vitest-coverable, extract the formatting into a pure helper, e.g. 'formatAgentPathChip(originalName: string, draftName: string, isNew: boolean): { kind: "new" } | { kind: "unchanged"; path: string } | { kind: "rename"; oldPath: string; newPath: string }' in agent-list-helpers.ts (or a sibling module). Falsifier: 3 vitest cases covering each kind arm.

title: Retire BrowseTab Skills view; extend search to match item names
description:
Origin: early design pass for the BrowseTab redesign (commit 4601457 conversation, decision #3). When the Plugins-only grid + Customize drawer landed in slices 3-4 (commits ec24a8d, 16e5e48), we agreed the Skills view's primary use case (per-plugin curation) was fully covered by the drawer, and the toggle could be retired. The blocker was that the Skills view's secondary use case (cross-plugin item discovery via type-to-filter) needs an analogue in the Plugins view before the toggle can go away.

What the Skills view does today that the new BrowseTab doesn't:
- Type 'pdf' in the filter box → see every skill across all loaded plugins whose name or description contains 'pdf'. The Plugins-view search currently only matches plugin name + description, so a skill called 'pdf-processor' inside a plugin called 'documents' wouldn't surface.
- Multi-select skills across plugins, then click 'Install N selected' → batch-install picks across plugins. Niche power-user workflow.

Scope of this issue:
1. Extend the BrowseTab.svelte search predicate to also match against entry.skills[].name/description, entry.steering[].name, entry.agents[].name/description. A plugin should appear in the filtered grid if EITHER the plugin metadata OR any of its items matches the query.
2. Surface why a plugin matched when it was via an item name (e.g., a small 'matched: pdf-processor' sub-line on the card) so users understand the appearance and can find the skill via Customize.
3. Remove the Plugins/Skills view toggle (browseView state) from BrowseTab.svelte.
4. Remove the per-skill multi-select selection set (selectedSkills SvelteSet, toggleSkill, installSelected, the 'Install N selected' footer button).
5. Remove SkillCard.svelte and its imports.
6. Update e2e (tests/e2e/app.spec.ts uses 'filter skills by name or description...' placeholder text — would change to 'Search plugins by name or item...').

Loss accepted: cross-plugin batch install via Skills-view multi-select. Power-user workflow; if anyone reports missing it, file a separate issue for a bulk-install UI on the Plugins view.

Estimated work: ~half-day for the search extension + match-reason rendering, ~half-day for the deletion sweep + e2e fixup.

Status: open, not blocking other work. Surfaces during normal BrowseTab use only as 'I miss being able to search inside skills'.

title: ScanRootsPanel z-index conflict hides global toast on scan failure
description:
When the ScanRootsPanel modal is open and a scan failure fires, the projectError feedback only surfaces via the global toast at +page.svelte (lines ~133-143). The toast has z-50; ScanRootsPanel also has z-50. Whichever renders later wins the stacking order.

Failure mode: user opens ScanRootsPanel, triggers a scan that fails, toast renders BEHIND the panel and is invisible. User sees no error feedback.

Fix: bump the toast to z-[60] (or pick any value > 50). One-line CSS change.

Detected by: PR #115 review (silent-failure-hunter M2). Rated minor — only manifests when the panel is open AND a scan fails AND the toast was supposed to surface the error.

See: crates/kiro-control-center/src/lib/components/ScanRootsPanel.svelte (z-50 on the modal), crates/kiro-control-center/src/routes/+page.svelte:133-143 (global toast, also z-50)
